summary: The implementation uses xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring() which is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. The manual post-parse checks for external entities are ineffective because the parser expands entities during parsing. The bandit:B314 finding persists across all 3 attempts with no resolution, and the attempted fix (manual attribute checks) does not address the root cause.
suggested_actions: Replace ET.fromstring() with a safe parser like defusedxml.ElementTree.fromstring() or configure the parser to disable DTDs and external entities using features='xml' parameter or similar
Alternatively, call defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() before any XML parsing to monkey-patch the standard library
Remove the ineffective manual checks for external_entity/doctype attributes as they do not prevent XXE attacks

evidence.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
evidence.tool_snippet:
20     try:
21         root = ET.fromstring(config_file.read())
22     except ET.ParseError as e:
evidence.context:
18:     
    19:     # Parse the XML document and extract the values of the <setting> elements
    20:     try:
>>  21:         root = ET.fromstring(config_file.read())
    22:     except ET.ParseError as e:
    23:         return {'status': 'error', 'message': f'Invalid XML: {e}'}
    24:
